A healthcare worker from West Africa, who worked in an Ebola treatment clinic in Liberia, has been admitted to Canberra Hospital with symptoms that could indicate Ebola. The woman is isolated in a single room, and doctors are treating her while wearing full protective gear. While health officials believe the probability of her having Ebola is extremely low and her symptoms are more likely due to other common illnesses, they are following strict protocols to ensure the safety of the patient, staff, and community.

Celtic Football Club has written to the Scottish Football Association to seek clarification regarding the refereeing decisions during their Scottish Cup semi-final against Inverness CT, particularly over a clear handball that wasn't penalized. Despite the controversial call, Celtic congratulated their opponents on reaching their first Scottish Cup final. The match ended 3-2 in favor of Inverness, denying Celtic the opportunity to secure a domestic treble.

A New York diner, Nicholas Dematteis, became enraged after waiting over an hour for his omelette at Bocca East during brunch. Dematteis then physically attacked a restaurant manager, grabbing him by the neck and slamming him against the bar and into an elderly woman outside, all while demanding a free meal due to the slow service. The incident, which included the use of expletives and a homophobic slur, led to Dematteis' arrest and charges of assault.

A restaurant in Leuven, Belgium, sparked outrage after charging a mother 36p to heat up her baby's feeding bottle, prompting her to share the bill on Twitter. The incident drew widespread criticism, with many users calling the manager "the stingiest boss in Europe." The mother's total bill, which included food and drinks, amounted to about £20, with the unexpected charge significantly increasing it. The restaurant later clarified that such charges apply only when customers are informed that the service comes at an additional cost.

Manchester City and Chelsea are vying for the signing of West Ham's left-back Aaron Cresswell, with both clubs seeing him as a potential homegrown option to bolster their squads. Cresswell has impressed in his first season in the Premier League after joining from Ipswich for £2 million, and his performances have caught the attention of top teams despite West Ham's reluctance to part with him. Additionally, Manchester City is considering other homegrown players like Danny Rose from Tottenham and Raheem Sterling from Liverpool to meet the Premier League's homegrown quota requirements.

Manchester United has offered David de Gea a substantial salary of £200,000 per week to stay with the team, significantly higher than the pay of other top goalkeepers like Joe Hart, Gianluigi Buffon, Manuel Neuer, and Iker Casillas. However, fears are mounting that de Gea may still opt to return to Spain and join Real Madrid for free next summer, a move that has been strongly resisted by United manager Louis van Gaal. Should de Gea decide to leave, Real Madrid is expected to pay United around £30 million.

Ian Bell, a key player for England and a long-standing member of Warwickshire, has signed a three-year contract extension to remain with the club until the end of the 2017 season. This commitment keeps Bell at Warwickshire for another five years, during which he has already won five trophies, including the County Championship in 2004 and 2012. Bell is a renowned batsman who has scored 48 centuries in first-class matches and will soon depart for England’s upcoming tour of the West Indies before facing New Zealand and Australia at home.

Tyler MacNiven, winner of The Amazing Race, created a unique and action-packed three-minute video to announce his engagement to Kelly Hennigan. In the video, inspired by action films, MacNiven and Hennigan portray a rescue mission, battling "villains" and escaping an explosion before the final scene shows them enjoying a boat ride under the Golden Gate Bridge. The couple's wedding is set for September 26, 2015, though the video does not specify the location.

Phil Rudd, the drummer of AC/DC, has pleaded guilty in a New Zealand court to charges of threatening to kill and possession of drugs. Rudd was arrested after police found methamphetamine and cannabis at his home in November 2019. He had previously denied the allegations but made the plea as the trial was about to begin. Rudd faces up to seven years in prison, though his lawyer plans to seek a discharge without conviction.

Rebecca Grant, a 40-year-old woman from Biddeford, Maine, became violent during her arrest after initially claiming she had been kidnapped and abused. When police discovered she was in violation of her bail conditions by possessing alcohol, she attacked a sheriff's deputy with a headbutt and tried to bite the police cruiser, damaging it extensively. Grant also threatened the deputies and their families, leading to multiple charges including violating bail conditions and criminal mischief.

A newborn baby girl, found dead in a garbage can outside an Ohio sorority house, was born alive and then suffocated, according to the coroner's report. The mother, a Muskingum University student, was interviewed but her identity remains undisclosed. This incident is reminiscent of another tragedy in 2002 where a baby boy was found dead in a dumpster on the same campus, leading to charges against the mother. The area has a Safe Haven Law enabling mothers to leave newborns unharmed at designated locations without facing legal consequences.

Former Brazilian football legend Ronaldo, reflecting on his career, suggests he would enjoy playing alongside his namesake Cristiano Ronaldo, despite his own retirement in 2011. Ronaldo, now involved with Fort Lauderdale Strikers as a co-owner, believes his time in top-level football has passed but would relish the chance to partner Cristiano at any club. He also expresses admiration for the current top players like Cristiano and Lionel Messi, praising their skill and goalscoring abilities.

Seattle-based CEO Dan Price of Gravity Payments is implementing a $70,000 minimum wage across his company of 120 employees, including himself, by slashing his own salary by 93 percent from $1 million to $70,000. This move, aimed at increasing overall staff happiness, will use 80 percent of the company’s projected $2.2 million profit for the year to raise salaries further over the next three years. Motivated by personal experiences and a study showing that higher wages significantly boost happiness among lower-income earners, Price’s decision highlights a growing trend towards reducing income inequality within companies.

Dikembe Mutombo, an eight-time NBA All-Star known for his shot-blocking and distinctive finger-wagging gesture, has been elected to the Naismith Memorial Basketball Hall of Fame. A key defensive player, Mutombo had a 19-year career in the NBA from 1991 to 2009, leading the league in blocked shots for five consecutive seasons. Joining Mutombo in the Hall of Fame class of 2015 are other notable figures including former college coach John Calipari and three-time WNBA Most Valuable Player Lisa Leslie. The induction ceremony will take place on September 11 in Springfield, Massachusetts.

A couple in Ohio faced charges of child endangering after they left their three-month-old daughter unattended in a McDonald's restaurant. The incident occurred when the mother, Rachel Jaajaa, initially refused to take the baby because it was the father's "turn," leading the father, Aaron Tate, to leave the infant alone in the restaurant. Both parents were arrested and charged after a concerned citizen called 911, and an officer found them arguing outside another fast-food establishment shortly after.

Derby County is considering Real Madrid coach Paul Clement as a replacement for their current manager, Steve McClaren, who is likely to be offered the position at Newcastle United. Clement, known for his experience under managers like Carlo Ancelotti, could potentially leave Real Madrid to take charge of Derby, though his future with the Spanish club remains uncertain. Derby is actively seeking a new manager due to McClaren's impending departure, with Clement emerging as a frontrunner despite the current interest from Newcastle.

Didier Drogba, the 37-year-old Ivorian striker, has announced his intention to continue his playing career beyond the end of the current season, regardless of his future with Chelsea. Drogba, who recently returned to the club after a two-year absence, is currently on the periphery of the team due to new signings and has not been offered a contract extension. Despite his limited playing time, Drogba remains focused on helping the team secure their fourth Premier League title, emphasizing the importance of maintaining concentration as they approach crucial matches against Manchester United and Arsenal.

British schoolgirl India Mayhew, aged seven, tragically died after suffering serious head injuries when she was thrown from a horse that bolted during a riding session in Matheran, India. The incident occurred on the second day of a family holiday in Mumbai, with Mayhew riding just meters ahead of her father. Local police registered an accidental death case, and the victim's parents decided not to press charges against the riding facility operator.

Joe Anderson, the Mayor of Liverpool, received £4,500 annually from Chesterfield High School despite not contributing to pupil welfare, which he justified by claiming to provide "kudos." After the school discontinued these payments in 2011, Anderson sued for discrimination due to his belief in public service. He lost both his employment tribunal case and an appeal, with the judge stating that the payments were a misapplication of public funds.

Sunderland striker Jermain Defoe appeared in good spirits despite his team's recent struggles, sharing a photo of himself playing basketball on social media. The team is facing a difficult situation in the Premier League, having only won once in their last nine matches. Defoe, who recently joined from Toronto FC, has not met expectations and is struggling to find form, with Sunderland currently battling against relegation.

Sebastien Pocognoli, a Belgian defender for West Brom, is expressing his confusion over being dropped from the starting lineup despite not receiving any explanations from his manager, Tony Pulis. Pocognoli feels frustrated but remains committed to the club and its fans, while considering his long-term future, particularly as the end of the season approaches. With his performances earlier in the season and his good standing with other clubs, he is contemplating potential moves, though he wants to remain in the Premier League.

Everton manager Roberto Martinez defended another penalty-taking mishap, this time involving Ross Barkley missing a 10th-minute penalty against Burnley. Martinez noted that Barkley had the right to request the penalty and praised his responsibility, though the ball was saved by Burnley's goalkeeper. Despite the miss, Everton won 1-0 through a goal by Romelu Lukaku's teammate, Mirallas.

Phil Haste, a 60-year-old businessman from Torbay, was fined £50 for not displaying a tax disc and a pay and display ticket in his car, despite the former no longer being required since October 2023. Haste refused to pay the fine and appealed, claiming the council's decision was "diabolical" and showed a lack of understanding of current regulations. The council clarified that they do not issue fines for road tax offenses and encouraged Haste to appeal.

Iker Casillas, Real Madrid's goalkeeper, has termed the constant media and fan scrutiny regarding his position as "morbid." Following a clean sheet against Rayo Vallecano, Casillas expressed frustration with the criticism he has faced this season, particularly from his own fans at the Bernabeu stadium. He emphasized that it is the coach's decision who starts, and encouraged support for the team's victories rather than individual performances.

England cricket star Moeen Ali, a lifelong Liverpool fan, has been named an honorary ambassador for Pak Reds, Liverpool's official fan club in Pakistan. This news was announced by Ali, who has Pakistani roots and was recently seen at Anfield with Liverpool players Raheem Sterling and Emre Can. Pak Reds, established in 2011, now counts Ali alongside other ambassadors such as former club doctor Dr Zaf Iqbal and tennis player Aisam-Ul-Haq in supporting the club.

In a small village of 3,000 people in Alderholt, Dorset, a mysterious Easter Bunny delivered chocolate eggs to every household on Easter Sunday, leaving one at each doorstep. Local residents, including Rob Wood and Tracey Feltham, expressed delight and surprise, with Wood noting it was a "lovely gesture" and "never happened before." The community praised it as a "wonderful act of random kindness," comparing it to a scene from the TV show "Vicar of Dibley."

The BBC is set to broadcast a unique two-hour documentary filmed at a snail's pace, depicting a canalboat traveling at a leisurely 4mph along the Kennet and Avon Canal in the UK. Produced without a presenter or narrative, the program captures the serene sounds of the journey, aiming to offer a refreshing contrast to fast-paced modern television. Filmed entirely in high-definition, the continuous shot showcases typical canal encounters such as swans and passing pleasure crafts, creating a mesmerizing and hypnotic visual experience.

Gareth Bale, a key player for Real Madrid, is sidelined for the upcoming Champions League quarter-final against Atletico due to a calf injury. Despite this, Bale posted a photo of himself on an exercise bike on Facebook, wishing his teammates luck and emphasizing his commitment to recovery. Real Madrid coach Carlo Ancelotti faces challenges with several key players out but remains optimistic, stating that the team has other options and is highly motivated.

A large wildfire ignited by a cooking stove forced the evacuation of 300 homes in Southern California, near the cities of Norco and Corona, on Saturday evening. By midday Sunday, authorities had contained 35% of the fire, which had spread across 1.6 square miles and threatened numerous homes. Despite the intensity of the blaze and its challenging terrain, no injuries or property damage were reported, and the evacuation orders were lifted by dawn on Sunday.

Charlene Mess, 48, was arrested on suspicion of murdering her husband of 30 years, Douglas Mess, 52, at their farm in Attica, New York. Her son reported his father missing after he didn't show up to work, leading to a large-scale search effort. Authorities discovered the body in a mound of manure on the property after a seven-hour search. Charged with second-degree murder, Mess remains in custody without bail.

During the Chinese Grand Prix, Jenson Button hit Pastor Maldonado, causing Maldonado to spin and Button to sustain damage, both finishing the race outside of the top 10. As a result, Button received a five-second penalty and two penalty points on his superlicence. This incident followed a challenging weekend for the McLaren team, with both Button and teammate Fernando Alonso struggling to secure higher positions.

The lawyers for the parents of Michael Brown, an unarmed black teenager shot and killed by a white police officer in Ferguson, Missouri, announced plans to file a civil lawsuit against the city on the following day. This decision came after a grand jury and the U.S. Justice Department failed to prosecute the officer involved, leading to widespread protests and a "Black Lives Matter" movement. The lawsuit is part of ongoing efforts to address systemic issues in policing and racial disparities.

Chris Smalling, a key defender for Manchester United, recently joined Twitter and posted a picture of a barbecue with his girlfriend, indicating his readiness for the upcoming match against Manchester City. Meanwhile, teammate Adnan Januzaj also opened a Twitter account, suggesting that both players are eager to use the platform to connect with fans. The article highlights the importance of these matches as Manchester United seeks to extend their lead and secure Champions League qualification, while Manchester City faces a challenging season.

A man named Sergio Barrientos-Hinojosa, who was allegedly intoxicated, had his 13-year-old son act as a getaway driver after he opened fire during an argument at a gas station. The son reportedly drove away as his father fired shots into the air. Barrientos-Hinojosa was arrested shortly afterward, and police found the discarded gun in a nearby yard. He faces charges including child abuse and was reported to be yelling racial slurs at officers.

In response to a ban on beef sale and consumption, the city of Malegaon, India, has required cattle owners to provide mugshots and details about their animals to the police. This move aims to help enforce the law and identify cow slaughter cases. The action follows a recent incident and aligns with a new state law that includes the slaughter of bulls and bullocks as non-bailable offenses. Beef traders and some citizens have protested these measures, while other Indian states have also implemented stricter regulations regarding cow slaughter.

In a goalless draw at Wheatsheaf Park, Chelsea and Tottenham Hotspur failed to overtake Manchester United in the Under 21 league standings. The match, which both teams had opportunities to win, ended without a score despite intense competition between the top sides. Ruben Loftus-Cheek of Chelsea was substituted after suffering a minor injury, while DeAndre Yedlin of Tottenham showcased impressive play, particularly in the second half.

In response to a lack of indoor space, over 1,700 freshmen students from Yichuan Senior High School in Shaanxi province took their exam in the school's playground. Students organized this unconventional arrangement themselves, even suggesting measures like periodically rotating chairs to avoid sun exposure. This outdoor exam aimed to test students' organizational skills while providing a novel and relaxing experience, and some saw it as a method to discourage cheating.

The Coast Guard cutter arrived in San Diego with 14 tons of cocaine valued at $424 million, part of a significant increase in drug seizures in the eastern Pacific Ocean near Central and South America. In the past six months, U.S. and Canadian forces have seized over 28 tons of cocaine worth $848 million, marking the most successful period for drug interdictions since 2009. This surge in seizures involved multiple interdictions and resulted in 101 arrests, with suspicious vessels being tracked and boarded by military or law enforcement agencies.

Jose Mourinho has hailed Eden Hazard as one of the best three players in the world football, alongside Lionel Messi and Cristiano Ronaldo, following Hazard's crucial goal that secured a 1-0 win against Manchester United for Chelsea. The Belgian winger is performing exceptionally well and is expected to be named the Professional Footballers' Association (PFA) player of the year. Hazard himself believes this is his best season yet, attributing his consistent performance to better managing the ups and downs of a full season.

Former Bayern Munich captain Franz Beckenbauer believes that Jurgen Klopp, the successful former manager of Borussia Dortmund, could be the ideal successor to Pep Guardiola at Bayern Munich. Beckenbauer expressed his confidence in Klopp’s ability to integrate into the club and suggested that Guardiola, despite recent successes, may depart before his contract ends in 2016. Additionally, Beckenbauer highlighted the importance of maintaining a fully-fit squad, particularly noting players like Franck Ribery, David Alaba, and Arjen Robben, for Bayern to compete against Barcelona in the Champions League.

Leicester City midfielder Esteban Cambiasso, a former Argentine international who won multiple titles with Inter Milan, sees saving the Premier League team from relegation as a significant achievement. Despite Leicester currently being at the bottom of the league, Cambiasso remains optimistic about their chances of staying in the Premier League, emphasizing that their campaign to avoid relegation is comparable to winning a trophy in his view. The team faces West Ham United in a crucial match as they seek to secure their Premier League status.

Jonathan Trott made his comeback to Test cricket after a 17-month absence due to stress-related illness, but his first innings was short-lived as he was out for a duck on his very first ball back. Trott was dropped in favor of Adam Lyth and moved to the top of the order, but the decision proved unsuccessful as he was caught at first slip on the very next ball. His return match against the West Indies began with England losing one wicket for just one run after only five balls were bowled.

Shannon Hayes, a 12-year-old schoolgirl from Capel Iwan, Carmarthenshire, believes her pet hen has laid the world's smallest chicken egg, measuring just 1.8 cm in length. This tiny egg, smaller than a 5p coin, potentially beats the previous record holder by two millimeters. To ensure the egg remains intact, Shannon has stored it in the refrigerator pending official recognition from the Guinness Book of Records.

Six Leeds players withdrew from the scheduled match against Charlton citing injuries, including four from the Italian president's signing list, causing controversy. Head coach Neil Redfearn and the team's medical staff were reportedly unaware of the players' supposed injuries. The situation has further destabilized Leeds, with Redfearn's position becoming increasingly precarious due to various issues, including the club's financial troubles linked to the banned president, Massimo Cellino.

Nicola Sturgeon, the leader of the SNP, has undergone a significant transformation in both her political and fashion styles. She has abandoned her previous boxy jackets and severe suits in favor of more flattering and modern outfits such as a fuchsia column dress and skirt suits that cinch in at the waist. Sturgeon is believed to have hired a personal shopper and stylist prior to the Scottish parliament election in 2007, leading to her current glamorous appearance that marks a stark contrast from her earlier, less polished looks.

John R. Lind, a 34-year-old hardware store worker, was found guilty of ejaculating in his female colleague's coffee as a prank, which initially made her think the milk had gone bad. Lind admitted to masturbating over her desk while she was away. Despite facing only a misdemeanor charge of indecent exposure, the incident prompted his colleague, Pat Maahs, to propose legislation classifying such acts as felonies.

Jamie Carragher has praised Chelsea captain John Terry, stating that he is the best defender to have ever played in the Premier League. Terry's exceptional performance contributed significantly to Chelsea's recent 0-0 draw against Arsenal, which brought them closer to winning their first Premier League title since 2010. Both Carragher and Chelsea manager Jose Mourinho emphasized Terry's outstanding defense, with Mourinho going so far as to call it the best performance he had seen from Terry in his six years managing the team.

Detective efforts have resumed in the 17-year-old disappearance case of Deborah Steel, 37, who ran the Royal Standard pub in Ely, Cambridgeshire. Her case was recently reclassified as a murder inquiry, and police have now dug up the patio of her pub in search of evidence. The investigation includes searches of both the pub grounds and a former residence, but so far, no clues have emerged. The current landlord has given consent for the investigation, and anyone with information is encouraged to come forward.

Manny Pacquiao, preparing for his high-profile fight against Floyd Mayweather on May 2, emphasizes the need to adopt a relentless fighting style to win the $300 million bout. Pacquiao, now an underdog, declares he must fight as if it were his last match, bringing a never-give-up attitude to each round. He expresses confidence in his ability to win and promises that the long-awaited fight will not disappoint the fans, stating that he will leave no doubt in the outcome.

A police horse named Charly was vandalized with graffiti at Venice Beach, prompting the LAPD to investigate. The graffitists used silver spray paint to write "RBS" and an arrow on the horse's flank, a technique typically employed on stationary objects. Authorities hope to identify the tagger using the cryptic signature, and are asking the public for assistance or any relevant information.

A 13-year-old boy from Medina, Ohio, stole $25,000 from his grandfather and distributed $100 bills to classmates and friends at his middle school, leading to a shopping spree. Most of the cash was spent, with only $7,000 recovered and some items purchased. Charges are expected once the investigation is complete.

Bastia's president, Pierre-Marie Geronimi, has demanded that Frederic Thiriez, the president of the French League (LFP), resign after Thiriez failed to shake hands with the players following Bastia's 4-0 loss to Paris Saint-Germain in the League Cup final. This latest incident adds to previous controversies, including an incident in 2012 when Thiriez refused to hand over the Ligue 2 champions' trophy to Bastia. Geronimi, in an interview with RMC radio, stated that Thiriez must leave his position if he wishes to remain as president of a league with 41 clubs.

Hayleigh Mcbay, a 17-year-old from Elgin, Scotland, played an April Fools' Day prank on her boyfriend David Clarke by texting him that she no longer wanted to be with him, only for David to reciprocate by pretending to end their relationship as well. The unexpected turn of events went viral when Hayleigh shared the conversation online, garnering over 12,000 retweets and 11,000 favorites. However, the joke turned sour when trolls began attacking her, prompting Hayleigh to defend the authenticity of the incident on Twitter.

A senior aide to US Senator Kelly Ayotte, David Wihby, resigned after being arrested for allegedly soliciting a prostitute in Nashua, New Hampshire. Wihby, who had served as the New Hampshire Republican's state director for a year and held several other local positions, stepped down on Saturday. Ayotte expressed shock and sadness over the incident, describing Wihby as a longtime friend. Additionally, Nashua police reported arresting nine other men on similar charges during the same period.

Arsene Wenger, after managing Arsenal for 1,056 matches since 1996, disclosed that he typically finalizes his starting lineup just before matchday, considering both scientific analysis and intuition. He spends the entire week pondering the selection, having a final consultation with the medical staff on matchday morning to ensure all players are fit. Despite this detailed process, his methods have led to significant success, including three Premier League titles and five FA Cups.

On a recent Jeopardy! episode, contestant Tom, a Freemason, gave an inappropriate answer to a question about puberty, mistakenly thinking the host had asked about the "age of consent." His response, "What is the age of consent?" shocked and embarrassed the studio audience and triggered a significant social media reaction. Viewers expressed shock and disgust, with many using the hashtag #Jeopardy to voice their opinions, highlighting how Tom's answer implied something deeply inappropriate.

Rafael Nadal started his clay court season on a winning note with a commanding 6-2, 6-1 victory over Lucas Pouille in the Monte Carlo Masters. Despite showing flashes of his talent, Pouille could not match Nadal's consistency and had to settle for five unforced errors. Nadal advances to the third round facing a potential match against John Isner or Viktor Troicki, marking an important step in his preparation for upcoming tournaments despite not having won the Monte Carlo title since 2012.

Auckland coastguards were surprised to receive alerts about an unusual annual fishing event featuring homemade boats made from household items such as a couch and tires. Despite some accidents, including a capsized boat and a bit of a paddle for three people, no injuries were reported. The event, which has been happening for years, aims to promote positivity and community engagement, with organizers emphasizing safety despite the presence of alcohol and strong winds.

Navinder Sarao, a British trader accused of contributing to the 2010 Flash Crash on Wall Street, boasted in an email to colleagues about potentially earning £1 million in volatile markets. He is alleged to have profited £27 million from the crash. Arrested last week, Sarao appeared in court on Wednesday but remains in custody due to the lack of a £5 million bail payment.

An official advertisement for the upcoming boxing match between Floyd Mayweather and Manny Pacquiao has been released, showing both fighters squaring off in a desert setting with the Las Vegas skyline in the background. Filmed in Los Angeles, the 30-second clip was produced by the marketing teams for both boxers and is set to air before the May 2nd fight, which is expected to break pay-per-view records. Sky Sports secured the broadcast rights in the UK and will charge viewers £19.95 to watch the event.

Rory McIlroy, the World No. 1 golfer, participated in the first Circular Soccer Showdown of 2015, defeating actor Jamie Dornan 2-1, marking a break from his usual golf activities. After a challenging start to the Masters tournament, where he ultimately placed fourth, McIlroy showed improvement in his game. Circular Soccer, a new football concept designed by medical professor Jim Dornan, aims to enhance skills and ball control, and has been adopted by FIFA and tested at Manchester United's youth academy.

Colleen Ann Harris, known as "Grandma Cokie," was convicted of first-degree murder in the 2013 shooting death of her husband, Robert Harris, in Placerville, California. This conviction came nearly three decades after she was acquitted of murdering her previous husband, James Batten, in 1985. During the trial, Harris claimed she had traumatic memory loss and acted in self-defense against an ongoing abusive relationship, though the jury found her guilty this time. She faces up to 50 years to life in prison upon sentencing on June 5.

Before their highly anticipated fight, both Floyd Mayweather and Manny Pacquiao took time to spend with their families. Manny Pacquiao brought his wife Jinkee and five children to Los Angeles, while Floyd Mayweather was accompanied by his girlfriend Doralie Medina. Both fighters are expected to begin their final intense training soon, with a focus on weight management, aiming to reach no more than 154 pounds for the official weigh-in.

Martin Guptill has been recalled to the New Zealand Test squad for an upcoming two-match series against England, alongside paceman Matt Henry, who is making his debut at the Test level. Coach Mike Hesson expressed confidence in both players' abilities, particularly highlighting Guptill's potential for influence in longer formats despite his success in shorter matches. The squad will be without Daniel Vettori and James Neesham due to retirement and injury, respectively.

During the post-game press conference of the NCAA Final Four, Kentucky guard Andrew Harrison used a racial slur and an obscenity directed at Wisconsin player Frank Kaminsky, which was captured by the microphone. Despite the offensive remark, Kaminsky showed maturity by dismissing the incident and accepting Harrison's apology. Harrison later tweeted his regret and apology, stating he did not mean to offend, and that he and Kaminsky had a good conversation afterward.

Scottish comedian Limmy shocked his fans by uploading a video of a three-inch house spider crawling out of his mouth and across his face, which lasted seven seconds. During the clip, Limmy maintained a grin while the spider moved from his mouth to his chin. While some viewers found the stunt amusing, others were grossed out. The incident sparked a mix of reactions, with supporters praising it as the "best thing" they had seen, while others expressed disgust.

Andras Janos Vass, a Hungarian man aged 25, was convicted of human trafficking and racketeering after he lured young gay Eastern European men to the United States through online dating sites like Planet Romeo, promising them jobs and high salaries. Once in America, he kept them captive, forcing them into sex work for up to 20 hours a day and threatening their families back home. Vass received a guilty verdict from a Miami-Dade jury and faces a potential 155-year prison sentence, with at least 21 years to serve.

Beijing International Airport is constructing Terminal 1, which will be the world's largest terminal when completed in 2018, aiming to accommodate the sharp rise in Chinese tourists traveling internationally. Designed by Zaha Hadid, the terminal will feature a six-tier, pillar-free design with flowing lines to reduce walking distances and enhance connectivity. The massive 700,000-square-meter space will serve up to 45 million passengers annually and reflects China's growing influence as a tourist destination, with outbound trips increasing from 10 million in 2000 to 109 million by 2014.

On a typically busy day at Bondi's Icebergs Club, the pool was unexpectedly drained to transform into a unique catwalk for Mercedes-Benz Fashion Week Australia. The fashion event, organized by the Ten Pieces label, featured a collection of unisex clothing designed to be worn in 20 different ways, showcased against the stunning ocean backdrop. Attendees enjoyed the pleasant weather and picturesque setting as models walked through the empty pool and along the breakwall.

The French climber known as the "French Spiderman," Alain Robert, climbed Dubai's 1,007-foot Cayan Tower without safety equipment, relying only on sticky tape and chalk for assistance. The 52-year-old took about an hour and a half to complete the ascent of the 75-story, twisting building, which was witnessed by hundreds of spectators. This daring feat, captured on video, is part of a series of similar climbs that Robert has undertaken in cities worldwide, often facing legal challenges and bans.

Former Manchester City player and manager, Stuart Pearce, supports keeping Manuel Pellegrini as Manchester City’s manager despite the team's recent struggles, believing he should be given more time to improve the team. Pearce attributes the team's decline partly to the loss of Ivorian midfielder Yaya Toure's influence, and suggests that the squad needs significant strengthening this summer. However, Pearce acknowledges that the club may need to reassess itself in the coming months.

On Easter morning, a married couple, Derek Lowe (38) and Tina Lowe (33), were struck and killed by an Amtrak train near Durham, North Carolina, as they lingered on the tracks. The incident occurred when train No. 80, the Carolinian, was en route from Charlotte to New York City. The train was delayed for approximately three hours due to the accident before continuing its journey.

Paul Gregory, a 51-year-old from Bedford, was fined £795 for falsely claiming he could only walk 200 meters to obtain a blue badge, while in reality he was an avid hiker and member of a mountaineering club. This deception came to light when council officials discovered a newspaper article detailing his 191-mile hike. Gregory admitted to the charges and was also ordered to perform 150 hours of unpaid work, pay a £60 victim surcharge, and attend 19 sessions of "thinking skills."

Manchester United are considering pursuing Hugo Lloris from Tottenham, but the club faces a significant obstacle as Spurs plan to demand a £35 million fee for the goalkeeper. This price tag is based on Tottenham chairman Daniel Levy's reputation for driving hard bargains and maximizing the value of his club's assets. Additionally, Spurs are determined to keep Lloris, especially from selling him to a domestic rival like Manchester United. The prospect of such a high transfer fee has already prompted hesitation from United regarding a potential summer transfer.

During the recent tax filing season, the IRS faced significant challenges due to an overloaded phone system that disconnected over eight million callers, and only 40% of those who reached an operator experienced long wait times. This issue was exacerbated by budget cuts totaling $1.2 billion since 2010, which led to reduced funding for taxpayer services and forced the agency to divert resources to comply with the Affordable Care Act. IRS Commissioner John Koskinen attributed these problems to insufficient staffing and inadequate funding, despite requests for additional funds for necessary upgrades and compliance with new regulations.

Former Top Gear host Jeremy Clarkson launched into a humorous rant at a charity auction, joking that his recent BBC departure allowed him to swear freely and even offering to trawl the job center for hosting gigs. This came after he was sacked from the BBC following an alleged assault on a producer. Despite the controversy, hundreds of fans still paid to watch his impromptu auction opening and jokes, marking his first public appearance since his dismissal.

Undercover police in London have identified Chilean pickpockets as the most effective thieves operating in the city, particularly targeting wealthy tourists in areas such as the West End. These organized gangs, often consisting of three to six individuals, use their intelligence and the cover of being ordinary tourists to successfully steal large amounts of cash and valuables. To combat this issue, plain-clothed police monitor CCTV and work to reduce pickpocketing in major shopping districts, though the criminals often move to other European cities to continue their operations.

Four children were playing around a parked minivan at an apartment complex in Las Vegas when an accidental fire broke out, resulting in the tragic deaths of two of them. The incident occurred at approximately 6 PM at the Arcadia Palms on East Sahara Avenue. Local officials stated that no criminal charges would be filed due to the ages of the children involved.

Real Madrid's players were greeted with a spectacular fan welcome to the Santiago Bernabeu stadium as they prepared for their Champions League quarter-final second leg against Atletico Madrid. The support included banging on the team coach windows and lighting flares. Javier Hernández scored the only goal of the match in the 88th minute, securing a 1-0 aggregate victory for Real Madrid and sending the stadium into celebration. Real Madrid now advances to the semi-finals, where they could potentially face Barcelona, Juventus, or Bayern Munich.

On NBC's Today show, a graphic incorrectly identified Saturday Night Live's creator Lorne Michaels as "Lauren," highlighting a significant naming blunder during an interview segment about influential individuals featured in TIME magazine's 100 Most Influential People list. Despite this embarrassment, Michaels continues to be celebrated for his role in launching careers through Saturday Night Live, which has garnered over 160 Emmy Award nominations. The article also notes his recognition from both American and Canadian honors, including a spot on Canada's Walk of Fame.

On Easter Sunday, the entire roof of the Korean Union United Methodist Church in Rahway, New Jersey, collapsed during a service, leading to the hospitalization of 14 people, including 13 with minor injuries and one woman with a serious head injury. The accident occurred around 2 pm when parishioners were singing, causing chaos and sending debris raining down on the crowd. No one was killed, which the police chief attributed to the day being Easter Sunday.

German Chancellor Angela Merkel spent Easter on the island of Ischia in Italy, where she faced cooler temperatures than back home in Germany. Despite the less-than-ideal weather, Merkel and her husband, chemistry professor Joachim Sauer, maintained their usual routine of luxury accommodations and health-focused activities. Merkel reportedly took Pilates classes and enjoyed vegetarian meals while avoiding the hotel pool due to the poor weather conditions.

Radamel Falcao, Manchester United's striker, highlighted Manchester City's Sergio Aguero as the biggest threat for their upcoming derby match. Falcao, who has observed Aguero's six goals against United since joining City in 2011, emphasized the importance of the fixture for both sets of fans. Despite not playing in the previous derby due to being sidelined, Falcao expressed his enthusiasm and commitment to the club, showing patience as he adapts to new systems under coach Louis van Gaal.

Andy Murray, currently training on clay courts in Barcelona with Jonas Bjorkman, enjoyed two consecutive visits to the Nou Camp stadium to watch his friend Lionel Messi and Barcelona secure a Champions League victory against PSG. Murray attended the match as the best man for his wedding to childhood sweetheart Kim Sears, which took place earlier in the month. This was the second time in less than a week that Murray had watched Barcelona play, following an earlier match where Messi scored his 400th career goal. Murray will continue his preparation for upcoming tournaments, including the BMW Open in Munich and the Madrid Masters in May.

Former tennis great Martina Navratilova announced the end of her coaching partnership with Agnieszka Radwanska, who was eliminated in the first round of a clay-court tournament in Stuttgart. Despite their December agreement to work together, the partnership did not succeed due to Navratilova's inability to commit fully. Radwanska, currently ranked ninth, is facing a challenging season, having lost in the first round of the Australian Open and suffered another early exit in Stuttgart.

On St George's Day, many prominent English footballers celebrated by sharing posts on social media. Harry Kane posted a photograph of himself as a child, while former England captain Rio Ferdinand shared an old photo from 2003. Current England defender Gary Cahill, along with other players like Nathaniel Clyne, Charlie Austin, and Peter Crouch, joined in the celebration by posting messages about representing their country. These posts highlighted the pride these players feel for England and its traditions.

Cristiano Ronaldo received a special "300" shirt from Real Madrid president Florentino Perez to commemorate scoring 300 goals in just 288 games for the club, making him the third highest scorer in Real Madrid history behind only Raul and Alfredo di Stefano. Ronaldo achieved this milestone on April 8 against Rayo Vallecano and celebrated with his teammates in Madrid. However, due to a knee injury, Karim Benzema will be absent for their upcoming match against Malaga.

Former Real Madrid legend and current assistant coach Fernando Hierro believes the current squad is capable of winning the Champions League back-to-back, drawing comparisons to the club's past successes in the 1990s and early 2000s. Hierro's confidence stems from the team's strength, confidence, and recent success, including a victory in last year's Champions League final. He emphasizes that the desire to win the European crown is embedded in Real Madrid's DNA, despite the challenge of breaking records and overcoming the hurdle of consecutive wins.

Chelsea are close to signing 19-year-old Brazilian starlet Kenedy from Fluminense, with an agreement reached on his economic rights through an agency representing several Brazilian players including Kenedy. Known for his performances at both the U17 and U20 levels of the Brazilian national team, Kenedy has attracted interest from top European clubs but Chelsea appear to be the frontrunners in securing his signature.

Mario Mandzukic, despite suffering injuries in the first leg of the Champions League quarter-final against Real Madrid, is expected to be fit for the return match at the Bernabeu. Coach Diego Simeone confirmed that Mandzukic participated in training and is progressing towards full fitness. Additionally, Simeone expressed confidence in Mandzukic's attacking partnership with Antoine Griezmann, praising Griezmann's recent form and potential for further improvement.

Italian authorities have launched a significant anti-terrorism operation targeting a suspected al Qaeda cell with ties to Osama bin Laden. The operation, which includes raids in multiple regions across Italy and one on the island of Sardinia, aims to arrest 18 individuals, primarily from Pakistan, who allegedly planned attacks on the Vatican and other targets. The suspects were under surveillance for several years, with evidence indicating their involvement in past bombings and attempts to recruit migrants for their cause.

Real Madrid are keen on acquiring Manchester United's goalkeeper, David De Gea, and are prepared to wait until next summer to make a move if negotiations with Louis van Gaal fail. The Spanish press refers to this as "Operation De Gea" and suggests that Real Madrid might consider signing Chelsea’s Petr Cech as an alternative if De Gea is unwilling to negotiate. Meanwhile, there is optimism about Lionel Messi's recovery from an injury, with reports indicating that all tests were positive and he is expected to return to play for Barcelona.

Ben Flower returned to the field after a six-month suspension for punching Lance Hohaia during the Grand Final, earning a standing ovation from the Wigan supporters upon his return. Despite the warm reception, Flower expressed surprise and humility, stating he did not feel deserving of such support after his actions. Coach Shaun Wane aimed to see Flower play more aggressively, but Flower emphasized the need to manage his emotions better, vowing to avoid being "dirty" while still being an effective player.

Wildlife-rescue volunteer Sandie Konopelski, 58, of Shiloh, Illinois, was tragically killed after responding to a call about an opossum on train tracks and being struck by a St Louis MetroLink train. She had a long history of volunteering with wildlife rehabilitation efforts and was licensed by the Illinois Department of Natural Resources. The incident occurred between Swansea and Belleville around 8 am on Friday, and service was delayed for about two hours during the investigation.

A Pennsylvania man named Kevin Rebbie, 56, was arrested after a 15-year-old girl discovered a hidden camera in her bathroom that had been used to film her and other minors. Over a four-year period, Rebbie allegedly sexually abused the girl when she was between nine and 13 years old, and recorded her undressing and showering. Authorities found 41 videos from the hidden camera, some containing explicit content. Rebbie is facing multiple charges and his preliminary hearing is set for May 1st.

Aaronessa Keaton, a 24-year-old woman from Phoenix, Arizona, was charged with manslaughter after a head-on collision in February 2014 that killed a 5-year-old child and injured others. Keaton, who was eight months pregnant and under the influence of marijuana and benzodiazepines, showed no remorse, stating "Sh*t happens." She was also on probation for a previous marijuana charge and was indicted by a grand jury, being taken into custody during a subsequent traffic stop.

FA chairman Greg Dyke returned a £16,000 watch he received as a gift from the Brazilian FA during the World Cup, after initially refusing to do so under pressure from FIFA’s ethics committee. The watch, part of a limited edition given out at the event, was to be auctioned by Dyke for Breast Cancer Care, but all the watches will now be auctioned for a Brazilian charity. Dyke had previously insisted he was unaware of the watch's value and intended to donate the proceeds to Breast Cancer Care.

QPR's center-back Nedum Onuoha is attracting interest from several clubs including Hull City, Everton, and West Ham United, with Hull specifically looking to strengthen their defense. Meanwhile, QPR is considering selling players to streamline their squad if they face relegation, potentially moving Catania's Nicolas Spolli to them. Additionally, Hull is interested in West Brom's striker Victor Anichebe, who is likely to be sold this summer.

The documentary film "Kid Kulafu" provides an insight into Manny Pacquiao's early life and journey to becoming a world-famous boxer, set to face Floyd Mayweather in a highly anticipated fight. Directed by Paul Soriano, the movie, which premiered in Manila, showcases Pacquiao's humble origins and his rise to fame through dedication and hard work. The film, released in Philippine cinemas and soon to be available in select U.S. and Canadian theaters, captures Pacquiao’s story from his early days in the boxing ring to his current status as a global sports icon.

Floyd Mayweather Jr. began his preparations for his upcoming match against Manny Pacquiao by swimming and performing resistance training. Meanwhile, Pacquiao trained by running up steep mountain roads. Both fighters are set to compete in what promises to be the most lucrative fight in boxing history on May 2nd in Las Vegas.

Benedict Cumberbatch, known for his role as Sherlock Holmes, was challenged to a game of table tennis by the renowned Chinese player Deng Yaping during the Laureus World Sports Awards in Shanghai. Despite playing with a wooden spoon to level the playing field, Cumberbatch still lost to Deng, who later used her standard paddle to win the match. The humorous and spirited exchange between the two stars, captured on video, became a hit among Cumberbatch’s Chinese fans due to his admiration for the sport and Deng’s legendary status in table tennis.

If Manchester City fails to qualify for the Champions League this season, key players like Sergio Aguero, David Silva, Joe Hart, and Yaya Toure could lose up to £500,000 each in bonuses due to incentivized contracts. The team currently sits in fourth place but faces tough upcoming matches, with only four points separating them from Liverpool for the final Champions League spot. Manager Manuel Pellegrini acknowledges the team's recent poor performance and the need for improvement, while Manchester United's Louis van Gaal remains optimistic about strengthening his squad.

The article describes a segment of the CNN Student News program from April 8, 2015, which covered various international and national news items including the U.S. and Cuba relations, California's drought, a flu outbreak in Chicago (specifically affecting pigs), and plans for a tunnel connecting Denmark and Germany. It also mentions the availability of a transcript for the show and invites viewers to participate in the CNN Student News Roll Call by commenting with their school details.

In Hong Kong, there is a significant demand for Jenny's butter cookies, with people lining up for hours to purchase them at a high price of $9 per tin. The popularity of these "little bear cookies" has led to counterfeit versions and opportunistic sellers attempting to profit off the craze. This phenomenon is not unique to Hong Kong, as similar food trends like cronuts and ramen burgers have experienced similar frenzies and eventual decline in popularity worldwide.

A 16-year-old student named Kevin Coulton from Manchester warns his classmates against a dangerous trend known as the "Kylie Jenner" challenge, where participants suck on glass objects to mimic the famous "bee-stung pout" of celebrity Kylie Jenner. This practice led Coulton to suffer from painful, lingering bruising around his lips and chin. The trend, now widely criticized for its potential risks, has caused similar issues for numerous young people who attempted the same method to achieve Jenner's look.

Bristol City is set to secure their promotion to the second tier of English football, provided they win their upcoming match against Bradford on Tuesday. Despite a 1-1 draw with Preston North End, Bristol City maintained their lead in League One and now need just three points to guarantee their return to the second division after a two-year absence. Meanwhile, Preston North End remains in pole position for promotion but must remain vigilant as they cannot afford to let their guard down.

President Barack Obama expressed his commitment to ensuring Israel maintains a military advantage over Iran, despite criticism from Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu regarding a deal struck between the US and five other world powers with Iran. The deal aims to halt Iran's nuclear ambitions in exchange for lifting economic sanctions. Obama argued that the deal offers a historic opportunity to stabilize the region by reducing the nuclear threat, while also assuring Israel of American support in case of an attack.

New Jersey Governor Chris Christie appeared on The Tonight Show and confronted host Jimmy Fallon about his previous weight-related jokes, which led to a humorous moment when Christie took Fallon's offered ice cream for himself. During the segment, Christie emphasized his unique identity, stating, "There’s only one Chris Christie. This is it," and indicated no plans to alter his persona for potential presidential ambitions, despite being prodded about future political moves.

Manchester City's captain Vincent Kompany, manager Manuel Pellegrini, and head of youth development Patrick Vieira attended the FA Youth Cup final first-leg, where City's Under 18s side suffered a 3-1 defeat to Chelsea at the Academy Stadium. Despite the loss, the presence of high-profile City officials was seen as positive, indicating support for the club’s youth development efforts. The match saw Chelsea take an early lead through Tammy Abraham, regain it just before halftime, and then secure victory with another goal late in the game.

In an unusual drug trafficking attempt, a suspect tried to smuggle $30,000 worth of methamphetamine wrapped in two condoms inside an Easter bunny, which was intercepted by police in Tulsa County, Oklahoma. The suspect, Carolyn Ross, was arrested when the package was delivered to her home in Tahlequah, and admitted to distributing the drugs to others. The police described the method as a "strange touch" and noted that they had intercepted similar packages before.

Swansea City have made a contract offer to Andre Ayew, the 25-year-old Ghana international striker who is out of contract with Marseille at the end of the season. Ayew, the son of former Ghanaian legend Abedi Pele, has caught the attention of several clubs including Newcastle, Everton, Wolfsburg, and Borussia Dortmund, and he is keen on moving to the Premier League. Additionally, Swansea are monitoring Schalke's Christian Fuchs, a 29-year-old full-back, who is also out of contract and known for his set-piece skills, as a potential replacement for Neil Taylor.

Jack Wilshere thanked Arsenal fans for supporting him and the U21 side during a recent match against Reading, which ended in a defeat. The 23-year-old midfielder, who has been out since November due to injury, hopes to return to the first team for upcoming matches, including the FA Cup semi-final against Reading. His appearance in the match marked his return from injury, though it did not result in a victory for the team.

Vincent Kompany disclosed that his mother prevented him from joining Manchester United at 17 by insisting he finish his education first. This decision allowed Kompany to later sign with Manchester City in 2008. As City's captain, Kompany expresses his passion for the club and their upcoming match against their rivals United, stating that while traditionally a one-sided affair, the game now offers a more balanced chance for victory.

Floyd Mayweather Jr. has met the welterweight limit requirement for his upcoming fight with Manny Pacquiao, weighing in at 150.5 pounds, which is just 3.5 pounds over the limit. Both boxers have been adhering to the weight regulations set by the WBC to prevent last-minute dehydration. To aid recovery, Mayweather recently posted a video of himself using a cryotherapy chamber, highlighting the importance of both rigorous training and proper rest. The eagerly awaited "Fight of the Century" is scheduled for May 2 at the MGM Grand in Las Vegas.

At Australia's Australian Reptile Park in Gosford, a four-meter-long king cobra named Raja recently produced a record-breaking yield of venom for handler Billy Collett, delivering almost 500 milligrams—enough to fill a shot glass and 40 times the amount of venom from a brown snake. This significant extraction, which Collett describes as "very dangerous," was a first for him and required careful handling due to the snake’s potential to hold its bite for minutes. The venom collected will be used for research purposes across the country.

During the second Test against the West Indies in Grenada, Joe Root scored 182 runs but was denied the chance to reach 200 due to a run-out caused by James Anderson's mistake. Anderson inadvertently failed to slide his bat into the ground as instructed, allowing Jason Holder to take the catch and remove the bails. Root expressed his frustration by throwing away his helmet and gloves, but later reconciled with Anderson when they returned to the pavilion for lunch.

An old hoax video from 2012 showing a wave rider surfing in front of Sydney's Opera House resurfaced on social media, triggering widespread discussion, especially after recent heavy storms in the area. Popular Sydney radio hosts Fitzy and Wippa shared the video, which was then widely re-posted. While some linked the resurgence to the storms, wave experts quickly debunked the video, citing the physical impossibility of ocean swells breaking that far inside Sydney Harbor.

Philippine boxer Manny Pacquiao trained by jogging around Griffith Park in Los Angeles to prepare for his upcoming fight against Floyd Mayweather, showing off his skills to spectators and testing his stamina. He also revealed the colorful mouthguard he would use during the $300 million match. During his training, Pacquiao was accompanied by his dog Pacman and participated in core-strength exercises and shadow boxing.

In a video recorded in North Carolina, three dogs—Sky the Husky, Sadie and Marshall the Springer Spaniels—create a harmonious vocal performance for their owner. Sadie initiates the performance by barking, which inspires Sky to howl in a melodious way. The trio then harmonizes, with all three dogs pointing their noses towards the sky as they howl together loudly. When the owner asks for quiet, the dogs reluctantly cease their performance, looking surprised and slightly disappointed.

West Ham United is considering signing Javier Hernández from Real Madrid as part of their summer recruitment plan. Hernández, who scored a crucial Champions League goal against Atletico Madrid, will return to Manchester United at the end of the season and could be available for around £7 million. West Ham also aims to sign other players like Pedro Obiang, Moussa Konate, and Fabian Schar, focusing on younger talent for their upcoming season at Upton Park.

A single-decker coach erupted into flames on a busy interchange at the M25 motorway near Dartford, Kent, this morning, with only the driver on board. The fire, believed to have started in the engine compartment, sent plumes of smoke across the carriageway and took over an hour to extinguish. Fire crews closed the A2 slip road to the M25 to tackle the blaze, which completely destroyed the coach. The slip road remained closed as authorities worked to remove the wreckage, causing traffic disruptions in the area.

The Brazilian construction company Grupo OAS, which owns the Dunas Arena in Natal and a stake in the Fonte Nova arena in Salvador, is selling these assets due to cash flow issues stemming from a corruption scandal and economic downturn. Following the 2014 World Cup, both arenas hosted fewer football matches; the Fonte Nova arena in Salvador faced criticism from local clubs and structural problems in one of its grounds.

Alison Saunders, the Director of Public Prosecutions, faced significant scrutiny after it was revealed that her legal adviser, Neil Moore, worked in the same chambers as the son of Lord Janner, a suspected child abuser. This connection, along with concerns about Lord Janner's alleged poor health due to Alzheimer's, complicated the decision not to prosecute Lord Janner. The controversy led to criticism of Mrs. Saunders, including from David Davis, a senior Conservative politician, and calls for an independent review or judicial review of the decision.

Charlie Austin, who has scored 17 goals for QPR this season, is being considered for an England call-up for upcoming summer fixtures due to Harry Kane's involvement with the Under-21 team. England's head coach, Roy Hodgson, has been impressed with Austin's performances and will continue to monitor him. Despite QPR's fight against relegation, Austin's consistent form has made him a strong contender for the national team, especially as Hodgson explores other options for his attacking lineup.

The return of Game of Thrones has led to a significant increase in internet piracy, with over 100,000 illegal downloads daily and seven million episodes illegally downloaded between February and April. This surge surpasses even The Walking Dead in illegal downloads, with Brazil being the top country for pirating Game of Thrones episodes. To combat piracy, HBO has announced plans to release the upcoming season globally simultaneously and launch a new streaming service, HBO Now, though it is currently only available in the U.S.

Former Manchester United defender Patrice Evra recalls that Sir Alex Ferguson predicted he and Ryan Giggs would become great coaches before leaving the club in 2013. Since then, Evra, now playing for Juventus, has taken some coaching badges and aims to earn his full coaching diploma. He reflects on Ferguson's insight, acknowledging the former manager's foresight and his own growing interest in coaching.

Research from the University of Southern California suggests that genetic factors play a significant role in the development of noise-induced hearing loss. Scientists identified a gene called Nox3, located in the inner ear, which determines a person's vulnerability to such hearing loss. This is the first genome-wide association study on noise-induced hearing loss in mice and could help identify individuals at higher genetic risk, allowing them to take preventive measures before exposure to harmful noises.

Susie Clark, from Evening Shade, Arkansas, found a 3.69-carat white diamond during her visit to Crater of Diamonds State Park, the only public diamond mine in the United States. She named it the "Hallelujah Diamond" after claiming that she found it right after praying for a discovery. This find was the largest of 122 diamonds found at the park that year. The park, known for its plowed search field, attracts visitors hoping to find diamonds, with recent rains and plowing efforts increasing their chances of success.

A magistrate has denied a suppression order to prevent the media from naming Brett Anthony O'Connor, a former head of child safety at Education Queensland, who faces ten charges of sex offenses against minors. Charged with assaulting two 12-year-old boys over 25 years ago, O'Connor's lawyer argued that identifying him would cause undue stress, but the court agreed to suppress victim and witness details. The case has been adjourned to the Campbelltown court for a June 10 hearing.

In 2011, David Seaman made a spectacular save against Sheffield United during an FA Cup match at Old Trafford, which he considers the highlight of his career, even surpassing his penalty-saving achievements for England. Seaman, who also won the FA Cup with Arsenal in the same season, emphasizes the importance of not underestimating opponents, as he himself learned when he retired unexpectedly due to a shoulder injury after his final game for Arsenal. He warns current Arsenal players, especially before their upcoming FA Cup semi-final against Reading, to avoid the same mistake.

Burger King will cover the expenses and provide gifts for the wedding of Joel Burger and Ashley King, who have been known as Burger-King since childhood. The couple, who announced their engagement with a photo beside a Burger King restaurant sign, accepted the offer during a Skype call. Burger King's spokesperson said the fast-food chain wanted to celebrate the union and hoped to "sweep them off their feet." The wedding is scheduled for July 17 in Jacksonville.

In February, Polish police found a mysterious man in Gniezno who claims to have no memory of his identity or origins. Described as 6ft3in tall and around 35 years old with ginger hair, the man can only communicate in English but recognizes some Polish and other languages. Police and experts are collaborating to identify him by releasing his pictures and trying to determine his accent's origin.

Raheem Sterling, a key player for Liverpool, has declined a £100,000-per-week offer to extend his contract, indicating he is not yet ready to commit to the club. Despite being one of Liverpool's standout players, Sterling's performance has slipped this season, scoring only six goals compared to Stoke City's Jon Walters, who has also scored six but might have more playing time or efficiency issues. His goal-scoring rate and minutes-per-goal ratio have notably declined from the previous season.

A large fire broke out at a former strip club location in Flint, Michigan, on Wednesday afternoon, causing significant damage to the building. The site, formerly known as The Body Shop, was not vacant and had experienced a fire there before. Local media reported that only the building and nearby power lines were affected, with thick smoke and large flames observed at the scene. The cause of the fire remains under investigation.

Ashley Young, a winger for Manchester United, celebrated his significant role in the team's derby win against Manchester City by joking about silencing their rival fans. Young scored the first goal and assisted in two others that led to a 4-1 comeback victory for United, breaking a 6-year losing streak at Old Trafford. The win boosted confidence in the team, allowing them to believe they could secure all remaining matches and finish the season strongly.

The article highlights a growing trend of back and neck pain among younger individuals, with nearly half of those under 30 experiencing constant pain. Experts attribute this rise to sedentary lifestyles and excessive use of small electronic devices like smartphones and tablets, which encourage poor posture. The British Chiropractic Association reports a significant increase in the number of young patients, even as young as 12, seeking treatment for back and neck issues. Additionally, the study shows that British adults spend approximately ten hours a day in a seated position and often fail to take breaks, exacerbating the problem.

David Villa supported his former club, Atletico Madrid, during their Champions League match against Real Madrid, despite moving to New York City FC. The game ended in a 0-0 draw, with Atletico goalkeeper Jan Oblak giving a strong performance. Villa, now enjoying his time with New York City FC and serving as club captain, also scored and assisted in a recent victory against New England Revolution. Additionally, fellow English player Frank Lampard will soon join Villa in the United States to play for New York City FC.

In a viral video, Casey Levi challenged his son Sam to eat a California roll for a $10 prize, but Sam couldn't stomach it and ran away nauseated. His younger sister Charlie, however, took the challenge and successfully ate the sushi, earning the prize and a high-five from her father. While many found the children's reactions endearing, some viewers empathized with Sam's dislike of sushi.

A six-year-old girl named Nurhayada Sofia tragically fell to her death after being dragged by an escalator at a shopping center in Malaysia. The incident was captured on CCTV, showing the child playing with the handrail at the top of the escalator before being pulled over the edge and disappearing. The accident occurred while her mother was on the phone with her husband, who later received the news that his daughter had died. An investigation has been launched, and there are questions about potential safety violations at the mall.

In a match between Barcelona and Celta Vigo, Celta Vigo's Fabian Orellana received a straight red card and a one-match ban after throwing a lump of grass at Barcelona's Sergio Busquets during an altercation. This incident occurred during Barcelona's 1-0 victory and resulted in Orellana missing Celta Vigo's next game against Granada.

Fiorentina secured a 2-0 win over Sampdoria thanks to goals from Alessandro Diamanti and Mohamed Salah, moving them into fourth place with 49 points. Meanwhile, AS Roma defeated Napoli 1-0, with Mohamed Salah scoring again and Miralem Pjanic adding the only goal. Juventus maintained their lead with a 2-0 victory over Empoli, while AC Milan won away from home for the first time in over five months. Other results included a draw between Inter Milan and Parma, and a late consolation for Cesena against Verona.

Adele Berry, a Vodafone customer from Reddish, Greater Manchester, was contacted by a call center worker in South Africa who had previously helped her with a bill issue. The worker subsequently sent her 18 flirtatious texts, calling himself her "future hubby" and sending pictures of himself, which Ms. Berry found disturbing and concerning. As a result, Vodafone suspended the call center worker pending an investigation into the incident.

During the eight-year Afghanistan conflict, the British Army expended 46 million bullets, including 27 million 5.56 rounds and 2 million 9mm rounds, costing over £200 million in ammunition alone. The high volume of fired rounds indicates the intense and frequent fighting experienced by British troops against the Taliban. The conflict resulted in 453 UK fatalities, with one veteran noting that the extensive use of ammunition highlighted the life-threatening nature of the engagements.

Tragic events unfolded in Dezhou City, China, where a 50-year-old man named Mr. Zhang died in a road accident that occurred at the same junction where his younger brother was killed eight months prior. Mr. Zhang was struck by a car while riding his three-wheeled motorbike and subsequently died in the hospital. The car's driver fled the scene without stopping, and the case remains under police investigation. The poorly maintained road, lacking proper signage despite being newly constructed, posed significant safety hazards, leading to calls for increased road safety awareness among local drivers.

Firefighter Cody Knecht successfully rescued six ducklings from a storm drain in Slidell, Louisiana, using a duck quacking ringtone on his phone to lure them out. The rescue, captured in a minute-long video, took 90 minutes for the first four ducklings and an additional hour for the last two. All the ducklings were eventually reunited with their mother on a nearby canal. This rescue was part of the station's recent activities, which included a previous rescue of a duck stuck in a chimney just weeks earlier.

An 11-year-old boy named Shiva died after allegedly being severely beaten by his head teacher, Lalit Verma, at Dwarika Prasad School in Railamau village, India, for stealing a pencil and eraser. Verma has been arrested and charged with culpable homicide not amounting to murder. This incident highlights ongoing issues with corporal punishment in Indian schools, as evidenced by similar cases of children dying under similar circumstances from excessive beatings by teachers.

Charlie Austin, a striker for Queens Park Rangers, initially faced a cold reception from teammate Joey Barton upon joining the team but later became great friends, thanks to Barton's apology and an invitation to dinner. Austin had a successful Premier League debut season, scoring 15 goals and being considered for the England national team. Reflecting on the upcoming matches against West Brom and Aston Villa, Austin emphasizes the importance of securing at least four points to avoid relegation.

A nine-year-old boy in Nanjing, China, suffered severe physical abuse at the hands of his adoptive mother, who whipped him with skipping ropes, metal rods, and water pipes for not knowing the story of Pinocchio. The horrific images of his injuries, including scars and bruises, went viral online, leading to the arrest of the adoptive mother on suspicion of child abuse. The boy, originally from a deprived rural area, was adopted by the 50-year-old woman after being given up by her cousin. Upon discovering the abuse, the school contacted authorities, and the boy is now temporarily reunited with his biological parents while receiving psychological support.

A 1960s hit band, along with infamous BBC presenter Jimmy Savile, frequented a notorious paedophile brothel in Battersea, London, according to a secret police report from 1964. In 2013, Detective Superintendent David Grey began investigating this connection during Operation Yewtree, after a woman reported being abused by Savile in the 1970s. Despite the police having intelligence about the band's visits to the same location, Detective Superintendent Grey noted that this alone did not establish a direct link or warrant further investigation at the time.

During Take Our Daughters and Sons to Work Day at the White House, a young girl named Anya Brodie complimented First Lady Michelle Obama, telling her she appeared too young to be 51 years old. This heartfelt praise moved Obama to hug the girl on stage. The event also included children of White House employees, participants from the Boys and Girls Club, and youths from D.C. Child and Family Services.

PSV Eindhoven's midfielder Georginio Wijnaldum has expressed interest in moving to the Premier League, favoriting a tweet from Sky Sports that linked him with potential moves to Manchester United, Arsenal, and Newcastle. Wijnaldum, 24, has scored 16 goals this season and wants to play in a stronger league, despite being happy at PSV. He cites positive experiences from speaking with teammates in English clubs and appreciates the quality of players and teams in the Premier League.

The British Army's newly established 77 Brigade, aimed at countering the Islamic State's online presence through psychological warfare and social media tactics, faces significant recruitment challenges. Despite its ambitious goals, the brigade will only have 454 troops upon full operation, falling short of its initial target of 2,000. Commander Brigadier Alastair Aitken has struggled to attract the necessary technical expertise and must now consider revising the Army's terms and conditions to appeal to a broader range of civilian skills. These issues have led to dissatisfaction among some reservists, causing tension within the unit.

In Chaohu, Anhui Province, a farmer named Xiao Qibin has become famous for owning a six-legged goat that was born 20 days ago. Initially born with four front legs and two hind legs, the goat has developed two front legs that are shorter and do not touch the ground. The unusual goat, which has been nicknamed the "spider goat" by Chinese internet users, is thriving and has become one of the farm's most playful animals. Xiao Qibin plans to allow the goat to grow naturally without attempting to remove the extra legs.

A garbage truck driver died after being crushed by his own vehicle in Footscray, Melbourne, on Saturday morning. The accident occurred when the truck rolled forward, pinning the driver against a pole. In a separate incident later that night, a car crash in Melbourne's east resulted in four people being injured, with one person dying at the scene and three others critically hurt and hospitalized.

Scott Stephenson, a 19-year-old from South Shields, UK, stole a deceased man's iPhone while the victim was found unconscious on a cold December evening, leading to his arrest. At a subsequent court appearance, Stephenson unleashed a profanity-laden rant outside the courthouse. He had initially been bailed for sentencing but failed to appear in a previous hearing, leaving his co-defendant to stand alone in the dock. Stephenson and his friend, Dale Walker, were found guilty of theft and handling stolen goods respectively.

Former football manager Harry Redknapp was verbally abused and thrown coins by Arsenal fans after leaving the Emirates Stadium following Arsenal's 4-1 victory over Liverpool. Speaking on Sky Sports, Redknapp recounted the incident, which occurred despite his previous friendly interaction with some Arsenal supporters. He highlighted how a small minority can ruin the experience for others and expressed concern about the level of abuse and personal insults directed at players and managers from certain fans.

Per Mertesacker credits the return of a "arguing culture" at Arsenal as a key factor in their improved form after suffering heavy defeats last season. This renewed willingness to confront issues head-on, particularly following losses to top-tier teams like Manchester City and Liverpool, has helped the team regain their competitive edge and is now challenging for higher league positions. Mertesacker emphasizes that this direct communication and accountability is essential for team unity and performance.

A 19-year-old Oregon teenager named Miranda Ashley Calvin was arrested on Sunday for allegedly hitting and killing a 49-year-old woman, Jerrie Ann Hornig, with her car and then fleeing the scene. Tips from the public led police to find Calvin at a hotel and arrest her. The case involved a white Mercedes Benz and resulted in charges of manslaughter, felony hit-and-run, and reckless driving. Hornig's son paid tribute to her memory, sharing a photo of her from high school and expressing his love and missed presence.

Liverpool defender Martin Skrtel has denied reports linking him with summer transfers to Wolfsburg or Napoli, stating he has no intention of leaving the Premier League club. Despite Liverpool's poor season and expected changes in the squad, Skrtel's agent confirmed he is in the process of finalizing a new contract with the team, with no offers from German clubs. Skrtel, who has made over 200 appearances for Liverpool since 2008, remains committed to the club despite his contract expiring in 2016.

On a Saturday morning in downtown Orlando, Florida, 20-year-old Britney E. Montenegro was involved in a street fight that began at a bar. After restraining her using an arm-bar takedown technique, police charged her with disorderly conduct. Despite being covered in blood, which was not her own, Montenegro appeared relatively unscathed and had only a minor abrasion. She attends Valencia Community College and hails from Queens, New York.

PSG's Lucas Moura expresses admiration for Lionel Messi ahead of their upcoming Champions League match against Barcelona, stating that Messi is his idol and the best player in the world. Despite the traditional rivalry between Brazilian and Argentine players, Moura emphasizes the importance of focusing on the game rather than the competition between nationalities. Moura acknowledges the challenge posed by Messi and believes that both teams will need to work hard to succeed in the two-legged match.
