A 49-year-old man named Eligah Christian, wanted on a $100,000 warrant for fraud and multiple theft and check forgery charges, attempted to evade Alaska State Troopers in a high-speed chase using a motorhome. After failing to stop when pulled over and engaging in reckless driving, Christian crashed into several patrol cars while trying to reverse direction. He was eventually stopped at a residence and arrested, facing additional charges of felony reckless driving and criminal mischief.

The 13 iconic glowing blue letters that illuminated Yankee Stadium for decades failed to sell at an auction hosted by Sotheby's, with the highest bid being from a Sotheby's employee who only offered $260,000. These letters, which had been part of the stadium since 1976, were purchased by Hall of Famer Reggie Jackson in 2008 and were expected to fetch between $300,000 and $600,000. Additionally, a New York Mets bullpen car from the late 1960s sold for $112,500, exceeding its estimated value.

Bob Shannon, the former trainer of boxing legend Ricky Hatton, has been charged with sexually assaulting a girl under 16. Shannon, who trained Hatton for a single fight in 2012, has also trained Hatton's brother and other notable boxers. The charges followed a police complaint received in the previous year, and Shannon is scheduled to appear before magistrates in April 2015.

Tottenham Hotspur is actively negotiating a new contract for midfielder Nabil Bentaleb to prevent interest from other European clubs. Discussions are progressing, but the club wants to finalize the agreement quickly due to concerns about Bentaleb's potential appeal to rivals. The proposed deal aims to secure Bentaleb's future at the club and could significantly increase his salary to around £35,000 per week, reflecting his growing importance under manager Mauricio Pochettino.

Kenny Dalglish believes that Harry Kane, who recently earned his first senior caps for England, does not need to participate in the Under-21 European Championship this summer. Instead, Dalglish suggests Kane should be kept fresh for Roy Hodgson's senior team in preparation for the European Championships in 2016. Dalglish argues that allowing Kane to play in both youth and senior teams would merely tire him out and that the primary goal should be to help Kane succeed in the first team.

Orlando City secured a victory over Portland Timbers, thanks to goals from Cyle Larin and Kaka in a tightly contested match at Providence Park. Larin scored early in the first half after deftly turning a Molino cross into the net, while Kaka converted a penalty late in the game to seal the win. Despite the efforts of Portland's Darlington Nagbe, the home team could not equalize, and Orlando City extended their undefeated away record to two games.

NBC journalist Jeff Rossen captured footage of an airport worker at LaGuardia Airport in New York performing push-ups on the runway between flights, despite having just finished loading a plane. Wearing his high-visibility jacket and gloves, the worker continued his routine even after completing twelve push-ups, earning him the nickname "the hardest working man at LaGuardia." The video gained significant attention on social media, highlighting the dedication and physical fitness of some airport workers.

Dirk Kuyt, the former Holland forward, is returning to Feyenoord for a new one-year contract, aiming to end his professional playing career with the club where he spent nine years from 2003-2006, including a stint at Liverpool and Fenerbahce. Kuyt, who retired from international football in October 2022, is expected to extend his stay at Feyenoord due to his fitness and desire to succeed, potentially becoming a long-term player like Ryan Giggs.

A truck crash on Interstate 93 in Boston's Dorchester neighborhood caused significant delays for morning commuters, forcing the rerouting of trains and the use of shuttle buses. Both drivers involved in the accident suffered non-life-threatening injuries and were hospitalized. Traffic remained backed up even after the truck was removed, leading to disruptions that stretched into the late morning.

Liverpool's goalkeeper Simon Mignolet returned to England after serving with the Belgium national team during Euro 2016 qualifiers, posing for pictures with pilots in an airplane cockpit before his arrival. Despite being included in the squad, Mignolet did not play in these matches due to preference given to Thibaut Courtois. Mignolet, who overcame early-season struggles and is now a key player for Liverpool, will face Arsenal in a crucial Premier League match.

Mike Ashley, the owner of Newcastle United and controversial figure in English football, has secured an 8.92% stake in Rangers FC following the ousting of his allies from the club's board. During the first meeting with Ashley since the takeover, interim chairman Paul Murray and directors John Gilligan and Dave King discussed potential ways forward, with the club hopeful of finding an amicable agreement. This development comes after the new board declined an additional loan from Sports Direct, opting instead for a smaller loan from another consortium to manage short-term finances while formulating a longer-term strategy.

Liverpool striker Daniel Sturridge was left in stitches by a humorous impression of former club legend Michael Owen during a promotional video for BT Sport. The video featured comedian Darren Farley, known for his stellar impressions of Anfield stars, delivering a particularly accurate portrayal of Owen that had Sturridge laughing uncontrollably. Despite the successful performance, Farley's dance move fell short of Owen's style, causing further amusement. Sturridge, however, remains doubtful to play in the upcoming FA Cup semi-final due to a hip injury.

A federal appeals court has upheld the conviction and death sentence of Clark Elmore, who was found guilty of raping and killing his 14-year-old stepdaughter, Kristy Ohnstad, in 1995. The 9th US Circuit Court of Appeals confirmed that the Washington Supreme Court's rejection of Elmore's claims regarding his constitutional rights at trial was correct. Despite the court's ruling, Governor Jay Inslee has declared a moratorium on the death penalty, meaning Elmore is currently serving a life sentence and may still seek a review from the US Supreme Court.

Swansea City is interested in signing Rudy Gestede, a striker from Blackburn Rovers who has scored 20 goals this season, valued at £7 million. The club is also considering other options such as Aleksandar Mitrovic from Anderlecht and Obbi Oulare from Club Brugge. Additionally, Swansea is looking to strengthen its left-back position, with Jeffrey Schlupp from Leicester City among the targets.

In southern Minnesota, about $70,000 worth of bull semen was stolen from an unlocked barn on a farm belonging to Daniel Weness of LeRoy sometime between April 1 and April 7. The semen, stored in vials within a $500 canister, has a range of values from $300 to $1,500 per vial due to varying bull genetics. Police attribute the theft to the valuable nature of frozen semen, which can be reliably used for artificial insemination to enhance genetic diversity in herds and reduce transportation costs.

Wesley Burton, a 36-year-old radio host and father of three from Berkeley, California, was tragically killed in a hit-and-run accident on Saturday morning. As he drove home from work, a white Dodge Charger struck his silver Mercury, after which the driver fled the scene on foot. Lucrecia, his wife and fellow radio host, has implored for any information that could lead to identifying the perpetrator. Police and community members are offering significant rewards for any leads.

Serge Gnabry, an Arsenal teenager and Germany Under-21 international, is recovering from a serious knee injury that kept him out of action since March 2014. He recently returned to play for the Arsenal Under 21s, logging 90 minutes in a match against Reading, showing promising signs of recovery. Gnabry is optimistic about his progress, stating, "I am feeling good, I'm feeling better and better," and is looking forward to returning to the first team.

Bastian Schweinsteiger, Bayern Munich's holding midfielder, is doubtful for their upcoming German Cup quarter-final against Bayer Leverkusen due to an ankle injury sustained in their recent match against Borussia Dortmund. Despite initial concerns, the club now considers the injury not serious. Additionally, Bayern Munich faces a growing list of injured players, including Schweinsteiger, as they prepare for crucial matches; this includes the absence of top scorer Arjen Robben and other key players.

Manchester City players, including Vincent Kompany and Gael Clichy, joined over 100 local schoolchildren to unveil a 64-meter mural at the club's Academy Stadium, inspired by young fans worldwide reflecting the positive impact of football. The mural, designed by Manchester-based artists Outhouse and covering the stadium's south edge, features global football scenes and the club's academy at its core. The event highlighted the club's commitment to community engagement through its charitable program, Cityzens Giving, which extends beyond the field to address global needs.

In an unprovoked attack, two 12 to 13-year-old boys sprayed a fellow 12-year-old student with WD40 in an underpass and threatened to set him on fire in broad daylight on Easter Monday in Newbury, Berkshire. While the victim sustained only minor injuries, police are actively searching for the suspects and have provided detailed descriptions along with asking for public assistance.

Arsenal are close to signing 16-year-old Argentine player Maxi Romero from Velez Sarsfield for a reported £4.5 million. The talented youth, compared to Lionel Messi, is expected to remain on loan at Velez for at least two more seasons before joining Arsenal. This move aligns with Arsenal's strategy of acquiring promising young talent, as evidenced by their recent signing of Krystian Bielik in January.

Dani Alves' agent has confirmed that the Spanish footballer has been offered a new contract by Barcelona, despite interest from other clubs like Manchester United and Liverpool. The full-back, who is currently 31 years old, has been linked with a move away from the club, but a new deal is reportedly on the table. Alves' current contract is set to expire in the summer, and further meetings are scheduled to discuss the terms of any potential extension.

Two school bus drivers, Steve and Sarah Nick, faced multiple felony charges after police raided their home in Davison Township, Michigan, discovering a large cache of weapons, including a 50-caliber machine gun, a sniper rifle, and 17,000 rounds of ammunition, which were allegedly purchased with stolen funds from Sarah's elderly mother. Also found were bulletproof vests, including one for their nine-year-old daughter, with a controversial patch reading "We kill for peace." The couple, suspected to be affiliated with the Michigan militia, could face up to 15 years in prison if convicted.

In Russia's Dmitrov district, a hot air balloon collided with a transmission tower during a training session, causing it to plummet to the ground. The incident, captured on camera by a nearby filmmaker, resulted in the balloon's disintegration and the basket hitting the ground, though fortunately, all three occupants were hospitalized with minor injuries and were reported to be in stable condition. The footage shows the balloon approaching the tower, the collision, and the basket's descent, ending with people rushing to assist the survivors.

Conor McGregor, a UFC fighter, declared that he would defeat Floyd Mayweather within 30 seconds if they were to compete. McGregor believes Mayweather's boxing skills are his only strength and would struggle in other aspects of combat. This challenge comes as McGregor prepares for his upcoming fight against Jose Aldo, while Mayweather is in the final stages of training for his high-profile match with Manny Pacquiao.

Dr. Alex Zhavoronkov, an anti-aging expert and director of the Biogerontology Research Foundation, predicts he can live to 150 years old due to advancements in medical science and a strict regimen involving exercise, supplements, and regular health checks. He avoids marriage, children, and material possessions to focus on anti-aging research. Dr. Zhavoronkov believes that within a few years, effective pharmaceutical solutions based on approved drugs will be available to help people stay younger and healthier, complemented by future advances in regenerative medicine and gene therapy.

Manchester City is determined to complete the £24 million transfer for Alvaro Negredo from Valencia despite reports that the Spanish club wants out of the deal, possibly to sign Radamel Falcao. Negredo, who had a loan spell at Valencia last year, scored against Levante without celebrating, which sparked speculation about his future. Meanwhile, City is also showing interest in FC Porto's Alex Sandro, with scouts attending Porto's Champions League match against Bayern Munich.

Former tennis player Greg Rusedski supports Aljaz Bedene's bid to be allowed to represent Great Britain in the Davis Cup despite representing Slovenia previously. Bedene secured a British passport last month but faces an International Tennis Federation ruling against his eligibility. Rusedski, who also switched nationalities in 1995, believes Bedene's inclusion would be beneficial for British tennis. Bedene, currently ranked world 99, could potentially debut for Great Britain in the upcoming AEGON Manchester Trophy.
